title: Drive recipe (the agentic-drive-readiness contract)
description: The single machine-readable source of truth for how a repo's drive surfaces are exercised, produced by /bn-runbook and consumed by bn-dogfood-verifier Step 0 and bn-review-lead's drivable-surface gate. The recipe is embedded in an instruction file (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) as a fenced JSON payload between HTML-comment sentinels (`<!-- bn-drive-recipe v1 -->` ... fenced ```json``` ... `<!-- /bn-drive-recipe -->`). It is parsed only through the shared validator (validate-drive-recipe.mjs); no consumer hand-rolls a parser, so there is no parser drift. The recipe fails closed: a malformed, absent, duplicate, or unknown-version block resolves to the consumer's pre-existing heuristic behavior or a typed skip, never to a guessed drive. Two conditional rules are part of the contract but enforced in the validator's code (this draft-07 subset has no if/then): `do_not_attempt` is required on any leg whose `tier` is `expensive-or-slow` or `no-dev-equivalent` (a leg that must never be executed must carry its cost basis and reason), and `observe` is required inside `drive` when `mode` is `trigger-and-monitor` (a remotely-triggered workflow must record how a dev observes/polls it).

recipe_schema_version.description: The schema version this recipe block is written against (R20). A consumer that does not recognize this value degrades to a typed `unknown-version` signal and falls back to its pre-existing behavior, never crashing or silently wrong-parsing. v1 is the value the validator currently recognizes.

last_validated.description: The freshness marker (R10): the run that proved this recipe and the repo state it was proven against. Consumers do NOT drift-check; this marker is the only freshness signal, refreshed solely by re-running /bn-runbook. A reader judges the recipe's age by it.

run_id.description: The /bn-runbook run id that produced and validated this recipe.

commit.description: The repo commit (git rev-parse HEAD) the recipe was proven against — the repo state the `proven` legs were validated under.

validated_at.description: ISO-8601 date-time the recipe was last validated.

paths.description: One entry per drive path. Each names a user-facing surface, its tier, how it is driven, whether the drive was proven or merely declared, an optional surrogate, and (for declared-cliff legs) a do_not_attempt note.

surface.description: The user-facing entry point this path drives — a route, a CLI command, or a triggerable workflow. Consumers reconcile a touched-by-diff surface set against these `surface` values (R18).

tier.description: Exactly one of the five dependency tiers (R2). `no-dev-equivalent` and `expensive-or-slow` legs are NEVER executed (R4) and MUST carry `do_not_attempt`.

mode.description: How the surface is driven. `trigger-and-monitor` MUST carry an `observe` instruction inside `drive` (R8). The v1 verifier drives browser/dev-server surfaces only; a `local-cli-process` proven leg is recorded honestly but treated as no-proven-path by the verifier (checker F3).

status.description: `proven`: the step was actually executed under the budget pass and evidence captured. `declared`: recorded with its cost basis but never run (R4). Only ran steps are `proven` (R3).

drive.description: How to drive this surface. `start` launches it; `observe` (required when mode is trigger-and-monitor, R8) is how a dev polls/watches a remotely-running workflow.

start.description: The command or smoke-journey description that starts/drives this surface.

observe.description: How to observe/poll the workflow from the dev machine when compute runs remotely (R8). Required when mode is trigger-and-monitor.

surrogate.description: The cheapest faithful-enough stand-in this path relies on where one exists — a fixture, a compose service, a contract mock (R9).

do_not_attempt.description: Required on any leg tiered `expensive-or-slow` or `no-dev-equivalent` (R4). Marks a leg a consumer must NEVER execute, with its cost basis and a human-readable reason.

cost_basis.description: Why this leg is off-limits to execute — non-zero money, a remote batch, slow wall-clock, or a credential/setup requirement (R4/R19).

reason.description: A human-readable do-not-attempt note explaining the cliff.
